goal: Land G6 (Platform) parallel-query aggregator with stable-order block aggregation in a new ucil-daemon::g6 module, exercised by the frozen test executor::test_g6_platform_aggregation that asserts parallelism, stable source-order, disjoint-block aggregation, sources_contributing accuracy, total_blocks / total_payload_chars correctness, partial-Errored resilience, partial-TimedOut resilience, and master-deadline trip.
plan_summary: Mirror WO-0073's G4 shape verbatim (and WO-0070's G3 before that), retargeted from Architecture (graph union + BFS) to Platform (simple aggregation per master-plan §5.6 line 535 'Simple aggregation — no complex fusion needed'). NEW file crates/ucil-daemon/src/g6.rs holds G6Source trait (DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam), G6Query, G6PlatformBlock, G6BlockKind, G6SourceOutput, G6SourceStatus, G6Outcome, G6AggregationOutcome, plus pub async fn execute_g6 (parallel orchestrator under master+per-source deadlines) and pub fn merge_g6_platform_aggregation (collect-and-order platform-context-block aggregator per master-plan §5.6 lines 524-537). The frozen test test_g6_platform_aggregation lives at module root in crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs (DEC-0007) supplied with three local TestG6Source impls (DEC-0008 §4) returning canned platform-context blocks; SA1..SA8 cover parallelism, stable source-order, disjoint-block aggregation, sources_contributing, totals, partial-error, partial-timeout, master-deadline. Pre-baked M1/M2/M3 mutation contract names file/line/restore for verifier. No new deps; async-trait already a workspace dep (added in WO-0068's Cargo.toml). No mocks of MCP/JSON-RPC/subprocess — real subprocess wiring (GitHubG6Source / GitG6Source / FilesystemG6Source impls calling F05/F06/F07 plugin manifests) is DEFERRED to a follow-up production-wiring WO (parallel to G1/G3/G4/G5 production-wiring deferrals). G6 explicitly does NOT do dedup, weighted ranking, or cross-source fusion (master-plan §5.6 line 535 is the load-bearing constraint); the aggregator preserves all blocks from all Available sources in input order and exposes structural counts only. classify_query/parse_reason re-export deferral from WO-0067/0068/0070/0073 stays deferred. Cross-group executor Group::G6 dispatch arm wiring is deferred to a follow-up cross-group-wiring WO (Group::G6 already exists from WO-0068).
scope_in: 1. NEW file crates/ucil-daemon/src/g6.rs (DEC-0005 module-coherence; one cohesive feat commit even if 700-900 LOC). Public surface: G6Query (query_kind: G6QueryKind enum { CommitInfo, FileContext, RepoStructure, GeneralPlatform } - default GeneralPlatform; max_blocks: usize - cap on total aggregated_blocks; max_chars_per_block: Option<usize> - per-block truncation when Some(N)), G6PlatformBlock { source_id: String, block_kind: G6BlockKind, content: String, length: usize, metadata: BTreeMap<String, String> /* stable-iteration order for testability */ }, G6BlockKind enum { CommitInfo, BlameInfo, DiffInfo, IssueInfo, PrInfo, ActionsInfo, FileContent, DirectoryListing, Other(String) }, G6SourceOutput { source_id: String, status: G6SourceStatus (Available/TimedOut/Errored), elapsed_ms: u64, blocks: Vec<G6PlatformBlock>, error: Option<String> }, G6Outcome { results: Vec<G6SourceOutput>, wall_elapsed_ms: u64, master_timed_out: bool }, G6AggregationOutcome { aggregated_blocks: Vec<G6PlatformBlock>, total_blocks: usize, total_payload_chars: usize, sources_contributing: usize, empty_sources: Vec<String> /* sources that returned Available with zero blocks; lexicographically sorted */ }, pub trait G6Source: Send + Sync (fn source_id(&self) -> &str; async fn execute(&self, query: &G6Query) -> G6SourceOutput).

2. Constants in g6.rs: pub const G6_MASTER_DEADLINE: Duration = Duration::from_millis(5_000); pub const G6_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE: Duration = Duration::from_millis(4_500). Per WO-0068 lessons-learned 'For executor' #2 + WO-0070/0073 lessons-learned: the per-source deadline is an UNCONDITIONAL const, NOT min'd with master_deadline. The per-source only wins on true global stalls (sleeper > 4.5 s); the master only wins on tight budgets (master < 4.5 s). Document the inversion inline at the deadline-computation site with a citation to WO-0068/0070/0073 lessons-learned.

3. pub async fn execute_g6(query: G6Query, sources: Vec<Box<dyn G6Source + Send + Sync + 'static>>, master_deadline: Duration) -> G6Outcome — fans out via the SAME poll_fn-based join_all_g6 helper shape as executor::join_all_g1 (WO-0047), g3::join_all_g3 (WO-0070), g4::join_all_g4 (WO-0073). Per-source futures wrapped in a private run_g6_source helper that applies tokio::time::timeout(G6_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE, source.execute(...)) and on timeout returns G6SourceOutput { status: TimedOut, blocks: vec![], ... }. Decorate with #[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.group.platform", skip(sources), fields(source_count = %sources.len()))] per master-plan §15.2 + WO-0073 lesson 'tracing applies to async/IO orchestration' + canonical naming convention from G1 / G3 / G4 (`ucil.group.<group_name>`).

4. Private helper: async fn join_all_g6<'a, T>(futures: Vec<Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output = T> + Send + 'a>>>) -> Vec<T> — same poll_fn fan-out shape as executor::join_all_g1 (executor.rs:1035-1067), g3::join_all_g3 (g3.rs:344-376), g4::join_all_g4 (g4.rs). Annotate the helper with #[allow(dead_code)] + a rustdoc citing the WO-0078 M1 mutation contract. Reason: WO-0070/0073 lessons-learned 'For executor' — the M1 mutation pattern (replace parallel fan-out with sequential `.await` loop) orphans the helper, and #![deny(warnings)] (lib.rs:152) converts the dead-code warning into a compile error. The pre-emptive #[allow(dead_code)] keeps the mutation atomic and one-step.

5. pub fn merge_g6_platform_aggregation(outputs: &[G6SourceOutput], query: &G6Query) -> G6AggregationOutcome — simple-aggregation per master-plan §5.6 lines 524-537 'Simple aggregation — no complex fusion needed'. Algorithm: (a) collect all G6PlatformBlock from non-Errored / non-TimedOut sources (status == Available); Errored/TimedOut sources contribute zero blocks AND are NOT counted in sources_contributing; (b) preserve INPUT-ORDER stability — iterate outputs in the order they were produced by execute_g6 (which preserves the input source order; the parallel join in execute_g6 returns results in source-input order via the index-keyed poll_fn shape); within a single source's blocks, preserve the source's emission order (do NOT re-sort blocks intra-source); (c) NO dedup — even if two sources emit identical blocks (same source_id + content + block_kind), both are preserved (master-plan §5.6 'simple aggregation' is the load-bearing constraint vs G4's edge-union dedup); (d) apply max_chars_per_block truncation per-block when query.max_chars_per_block is Some(N): if block.content.len() > N, truncate to char_indices().nth(N).map(|(i,_)| &block.content[..i]).unwrap_or(&block.content) AND adjust block.length to the truncated char-count (use char-boundary-safe truncation, NOT byte-slice truncation); leave content unchanged when None; (e) cap at query.max_blocks total (truncate aggregated_blocks if it exceeds the cap; preserve input order while truncating from the tail); (f) compute totals: total_blocks = aggregated_blocks.len(); total_payload_chars = sum of aggregated_blocks[i].length (post-truncation, post-cap); sources_contributing = count of distinct source_ids that contributed at least one block to aggregated_blocks (post-cap; a source whose blocks were all truncated by max_blocks no longer counts); empty_sources = lexicographically-sorted Vec<String> of source_ids whose status == Available but blocks.is_empty().

6. Add `pub mod g6;` to crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs alphabetically AFTER `pub mod g4;` (line 160) and BEFORE `pub mod lancedb_indexer;` (line 161). Per WO-0068/0070/0073 lessons-learned 'For planner': the alphabetical convention wins — `g6` slots between `g4` and `lancedb_indexer` (g5 will land later in P3-W10-F04's solo WO and slot between g4 and g6 at that time; for now the gap is intentional). The line range becomes 159-162 after insertion (g3, g4, g6, lancedb_indexer).

7. Add a #[rustfmt::skip] pub use block in crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs for the new types, alphabetically positioned AFTER the existing `pub use g4::...` block (line 193) and BEFORE `pub use lancedb_indexer::...` block (line 195). Re-export: G6Source, G6Query, G6QueryKind, G6PlatformBlock, G6BlockKind, G6SourceOutput, G6SourceStatus, G6Outcome, G6AggregationOutcome, execute_g6, merge_g6_platform_aggregation, G6_MASTER_DEADLINE, G6_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE.

8. NEW frozen test crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs::test_g6_platform_aggregation — append after the existing test_g4_architecture_query section (executor.rs line ~3790+ region; the WO-0073 test ends near line ~4459 — append below). Place at MODULE ROOT inside the executor.rs file (NOT under `mod tests { ... }`) per DEC-0007 + WO-0068/0070/0073 lessons. Signature: #[cfg(test)] #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines, clippy::missing_panics_doc)] #[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 4)] pub async fn test_g6_platform_aggregation() {}. The substring selector `executor::test_g6_platform_aggregation` MUST resolve cleanly (NOT `executor::tests::test_g6_platform_aggregation`). This is the literal acceptance_tests selector frozen in feature-list.json for P3-W10-F08. Imports: use crate::g6::{...}; — types come from the daemon crate.

9. Inside test_g6_platform_aggregation: define enum TestBehaviour { ReturnBlocks(Vec<G6PlatformBlock>), Sleep(Duration, Vec<G6PlatformBlock>), Error(String), LongSleep(Duration), Empty }. Define struct TestG6Source { id: String, behaviour: TestBehaviour }. Implement #[async_trait::async_trait] G6Source for TestG6Source: source_id returns &self.id, execute matches on behaviour and returns appropriate G6SourceOutput. Per DEC-0008 §4 these are UCIL-internal trait impls (NOT mocks of any external wire format) — the same shape as WO-0047's TestG1Source, WO-0070's TestG3Source, WO-0073's TestG4Source. Helper fn make_block(source: &str, kind: G6BlockKind, content: &str) -> G6PlatformBlock for ergonomic construction (length = content.chars().count(); metadata = BTreeMap::new()).

10. Frozen-test sub-assertions (target ≥7 SAs, each carrying `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ...; right: ...` panic body per DEC-0007). SA1 — Parallelism: 3 sources each Sleep(200 ms, ...) must return wall_elapsed_ms in [180, 500) (lower-bound proves at least one source's sleep elapsed; upper-bound TIGHTENED to 500 ms per WO-0070/0073 lessons-learned 'For planner' #1 — compute upper bound from max_per_source_sleep_ms * (N_sources / 2) = 200 * (3/2) = 300 ms target, with a 200 ms noise margin → 500 ms ceiling. The literal scope_in upper bound MUST flip the M1 sequential-execution mutation: 3×200 ms = 600 ms sequential serially-await total > 500 ms ceiling → SA1 panic). SA2 — Stable source ordering: source IDs in ["git", "github", "filesystem"] order (input order, NOT alphabetical); each source returns 1 block; merge.aggregated_blocks contains exactly 3 blocks where aggregated_blocks[0].source_id == "git" AND aggregated_blocks[1].source_id == "github" AND aggregated_blocks[2].source_id == "filesystem" (input order preserved). SA3 — Disjoint blocks aggregated (no dedup): source A returns [Block(CommitInfo, "commit-1"), Block(BlameInfo, "blame-1")] AND source B returns [Block(IssueInfo, "issue-1")]; merge.aggregated_blocks contains exactly 3 blocks (intra-source order preserved within each source's contribution; A's 2 blocks come before B's 1 block); merge.sources_contributing == 2. SA4 — sources_contributing accuracy with empty source: source A returns [Block(CommitInfo, "only")], source B returns [], source C returns [Block(IssueInfo, "third")]; merge.aggregated_blocks.len() == 2 (only A and C contribute), merge.sources_contributing == 2 (NOT 3 — B is empty), merge.empty_sources == vec!["src-b"] (the lex-sorted source_id of the empty Available source). SA5 — total_blocks AND total_payload_chars correctness: 2 sources each return 2 blocks of content_lengths {10, 20} and {30, 40} → merge.total_blocks == 4, merge.total_payload_chars == 100 (10+20+30+40). NO truncation applied (max_chars_per_block = None, max_blocks = 1000). SA6 — Partial-Errored: 1 of 3 sources returns Errored, 2 return blocks; outcome.results carries exactly 1 Errored + 2 Available, AND merge proceeds successfully where merge.aggregated_blocks contains ONLY blocks from the 2 Available sources (count = sum of those two sources' blocks); merge.sources_contributing == 2 (NOT 3 — Errored does NOT count). SA7 — Partial-TimedOut: 1 of 3 sources LongSleep(6 s) (> G6_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE = 4.5 s); outcome.results carries exactly 1 TimedOut + 2 Available, wall_elapsed_ms < 5500 ms (per-source 4.5 s ceiling fires before master 5 s); merge.aggregated_blocks contains ONLY blocks from the 2 Available sources; merge.sources_contributing == 2 (TimedOut does NOT count). SA8 — Master-deadline-trip: master_deadline=100 ms, all 3 sources Sleep(7 s, ...) → outcome.master_timed_out == true AND wall_elapsed_ms < 2000 ms (master deadline trips before per-source). Per WO-0068/0070/0073 lessons-learned 'For planner': this SA is COMPATIBLE with the unconditional-const per-source deadline — the per-source CONST never gets shortened to master_deadline.

11. Pre-baked M1/M2/M3 mutation contract (verifier applies in-place via Edit, NOT git stash; takes md5sum snapshot first; runs targeted cargo test selector; observes SA-tagged panic; restores via git checkout -- <file>; confirms md5sum match). M1 — Sequential execution: in crates/ucil-daemon/src/g6.rs execute_g6, replace the parallel join (`join_all_g6(futures)` call) with a sequential `let mut outputs = Vec::with_capacity(sources.len()); for source in sources.into_iter() { let r = run_g6_source(source, &query).await; outputs.push(r); }` loop (or equivalent). The orphaned join_all_g6 helper carries #[allow(dead_code)] per scope_in #4, so the mutation compiles cleanly. Targeted selector: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon executor::test_g6_platform_aggregation`. Expected: SA1 (Parallelism) panics with `(SA1) parallel wall < 500 ms; left: 6XX, right: 500`. Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/g6.rs`. M2 — Reverse aggregation order: in merge_g6_platform_aggregation, after collecting blocks from outputs into a Vec<G6PlatformBlock>, append `.into_iter().rev().collect()` (or equivalent reverse). The 2-character mutation idiom: change `.collect()` → `.rev().collect()` at the aggregation-output line. Targeted selector: same as M1. Expected: SA2 (Stable source ordering) panics with `(SA2) aggregated_blocks[0].source_id == "git"; left: "filesystem", right: "git"` (the first slot now holds the last source's block due to reversal). M3 — Inverted Available filter: in merge_g6_platform_aggregation, replace the `if matches!(output.status, G6SourceStatus::Available)` block-inclusion check with `if !matches!(output.status, G6SourceStatus::Available)` (or equivalent — the `==` → `!=` flip-bit shape per WO-0073/0074 M2 precedent). Targeted selector: same as M1. Expected: SA6 (Partial-Errored) panics with `(SA6) sources_contributing == 2; left: 1, right: 2` — the Errored source's empty blocks vector contributes zero blocks (so aggregated_blocks ends up empty), and the two Available sources' blocks are filtered OUT (because the inverted check excludes Available); the lone source-with-blocks remaining is the Errored source's empty blocks list, so sources_contributing collapses to 1 (or 0 depending on inverted accounting). The verifier applies each mutation in isolation (one at a time), confirms targeted SA fails, restores file, runs full test to confirm restoration, then proceeds to next mutation.

12. NEW file scripts/verify/P3-W10-F08.sh (chmod +x). Bash strict mode (set -euo pipefail). Exits 0 iff `cargo test -p ucil-daemon executor::test_g6_platform_aggregation --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | tee /tmp/p3-w10-f08-test.log` exits 0 AND grep -qE '^test executor::test_g6_platform_aggregation \.\.\. ok$' /tmp/p3-w10-f08-test.log. Echoes `[PASS] P3-W10-F08: G6 platform parallel-aggregation frozen test green` on success, `[FAIL] P3-W10-F08: ...` on failure. Mirrors scripts/verify/P3-W9-F09.sh (G4) shape from WO-0073. NO mocks. NO env-gated short-circuits — the test is fully synthetic (TestG6Source local impls; no subprocess, no API key, no plugin manager invocation).

Test-helper structs named TestG6Source / SleepingG6Source / ErroringG6Source under #[cfg(test)] in executor.rs are EXEMPT (precedent: WO-0048 line 363 #[cfg(test)] carve-out, reaffirmed by WO-0068/0070/0073 lessons-learned). The exemption applies only inside the executor.rs module-root test region; production code in g6.rs MUST be clean.

Use the SAME poll_fn-based join_all_g6 helper shape as executor::join_all_g1 / g3::join_all_g3 / g4::join_all_g4 — no extra dep needed (no JoinSet, no futures::future::join_all).

15. Lessons applied (cite in commit bodies for WO-0078): #1 WO-0067/0068/0069/0070/0072/0073 'pre-bake M1/M2/M3 mutation contract'; #2 WO-0068/0070/0073 'frozen-test at module root in executor.rs, NOT under mod tests {}'; #3 WO-0068/0070/0073 'per-source deadline is unconditional const, NOT min'd with master_deadline'; #4 WO-0067 + WO-0073 'master-plan §15.2 tracing spans apply to async/IO orchestration with canonical span name `ucil.group.<group_name>`'; #5 WO-0068/0070/0073 'alphabetical placement wins for pub mod / pub use blocks'; #6 WO-0067/0068/0069/0070/0073 'DEC-0007 SA-numbered panic bodies for mutation-diagnosis trivia'; #7 WO-0069/0073 'word-ban grep production-side; test impls named with Test/G6Source suffix exempt'; #8 WO-0068/0070/0073 'no mocks of MCP/JSON-RPC/subprocess — UCIL-owned DI seam, real subprocess wiring deferred to a production-wiring WO'; #9 WO-0070/0073 'pre-emptively place #[allow(dead_code)] on the parallel-join helper so M1 mutation compiles cleanly under #![deny(warnings)]'; #10 WO-0070/0073 'compute parallelism dual-bound upper limit from max_per_source_sleep_ms * (N_sources / 2) — NOT a round-number margin' (SA1 ceiling tightened to 500 ms for 3×200 ms sources); #11 WO-0070/0073 'multi-thread tokio test flavor for parallelism observability' (SA1 needs concurrent execution, not single-thread serialization); #12 WO-0070/0073 'AC25 reworded to git log feat ^main --merges empty rather than `feat..main = 0` — workflow-timing tolerant'; #13 WO-0073/0074 'M2 mutation as 2-character flip on existing predicate (`==` → `!=`, or `.collect()` → `.rev().collect()`) for cleanest verifier-applicable patch shape'.

The phase-3 effectiveness-evaluator + gate-check child agents commit refresh artefacts and harness-config escalations to the feat branch during gate execution. WO-0067 scope_in #42 / WO-0068 / WO-0069 / WO-0070 / WO-0072 / WO-0073 / WO-0074 / WO-0075 / WO-0076 / WO-0077 are the working templates.

22. Master-plan §5.6 'simple aggregation' is the load-bearing constraint. NO dedup of identical blocks across sources (contrast with G4's edge-union dedup); NO weighted ranking (contrast with G2's RRF); NO source-authority hierarchy (deferred to P3-W10-F10's conflict resolution work). The aggregator preserves ALL blocks from ALL Available sources in input order. SA3 'Disjoint blocks aggregated (no dedup)' explicitly tests the no-dedup contract — if a future planner wants to add dedup, it must be a separate feature with a separate WO and an ADR overriding §5.6's 'simple aggregation' wording.
scope_out: 1. Real subprocess wiring of GitHubG6Source / GitG6Source / FilesystemG6Source impls that talk to the F05/F06/F07 plugin manifests (via PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout-equivalent runtime invocation + tools/call dispatch over MCP wire format). Deferred to a follow-up production-wiring WO that bundles G6 group-executor wiring INTO the cross-group adapter (parallels DEC-0008 §4 deferred-real-impls-of-G1Source pattern; same as G3's deferred CodebaseMemoryG3Source / Mem0G3Source and G4's deferred CodeGraphContextG4Source / LSPCallHierarchyG4Source). F08 ships the trait + orchestrator + aggregator + frozen test with TestG6Source local impls only.
2. Docker MCP / Terraform MCP / kubectl MCP / Playwright MCP plugin manifests (master-plan §4.6 P1/P2). Each is a separate feature with its own WO outside Phase 3 scope.

3. Wiring G6 into the cross-group executor (cross_group::execute_cross_group dispatching to G6 via Group::G6 enum variant). Deferred to a follow-up cross-group-wiring WO; Group::G6 already exists from WO-0068.

4. classify_query / parse_reason re-exports from crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs deferred since WO-0067/0068/0070/0073. Continues deferred pending the daemon-side classify-then-dispatch pipeline WO; F08 does not need them (the test exercises execute_g6 + merge_g6_platform_aggregation directly).
5. Daemon-side server.rs / lifecycle.rs wiring that routes incoming MCP queries through classify_query before group dispatch. Deferred to a follow-up daemon-side WO.

P3-W10-F09 'Quality-maximalist response assembly' / P3-W10-F10 'Conflict resolution: source authority hierarchy' / P3-W10-F11 'Bonus context selector' / P3-W10-F12 'Multi-tier query merging' / P3-W10-F13 'Warm processors' — separate Phase-3 WOs each. F08 ships only the G6 group-executor producer surface.

12. Existing G1/G2/G3/G4/cross-group code paths — read-only this WO (per scope_in #19). The G1/G3/G4 enrich + execute + fuse/merge surfaces stay at HEAD.
13. The 22 MCP tools' get_architecture / trace_dependencies / blast_radius routing (P3-W10-F16/F17/F18) — these consume G4 surfaces, NOT G6. F08 ships the producer surface only.

14. KG platform relations as a G6Source / cross-source dedup of platform blocks — that wiring depends on conflict-resolution (P3-W10-F10) landing first; deferred. Master-plan §5.6 explicitly says 'simple aggregation — no complex fusion needed' so cross-source dedup is NOT a Phase 3 deliverable for G6.

15. The standing-protocol coverage workaround for `coverage-gate.sh ucil-{core,daemon,embeddings} ...` failing under sccache RUSTC_WRAPPER. This WO inherits the AC23 substantive-measurement protocol but does NOT advance the harness-improvement debt. Bucket B / Bucket D candidate (now 33+ WOs deep, urgency lower than previously assessed per WO-0072/0073/0074/0075/0076/0077 verifier reports).
